# t21 — per-edge post: runs after persist; failure → move kept + rendered + surfaced.
exec mtt init
cp post.yaml .mtt/config.yaml
exec mtt types
stdout 'task'

exec mtt add 'a task'
stdout 'created t1'

# a passing post runs and its output is visible (-v streams gate/post output)
exec mtt start t1 -v
stdout 't1: tbd → speccing'
stderr 'POSTRAN'

# a failing post → move is KEPT and RENDERED, plus the recovery block surfaced (t28):
# the move IS saved, and the exact remaining post commands to finish by hand.
! exec mtt submit t1
stdout 't1: speccing → review'
stderr 'the status change IS saved'
stderr 'do NOT re-run the move'
stderr '^  false$'
exec mtt show t1
stdout '\[review\]'

# --json on the failing post STILL emits the task object AND prints the recovery block
# on stderr (t28: both modes — an agent driving --json must still see how to finish).
exec mtt add 'b task'
exec mtt start t2
! exec mtt submit t2 --json
stdout '"status": *"review"'
stderr 'the status change IS saved'
stderr '^  false$'
exec mtt show t2
stdout '\[review\]'

# --no-run skips BOTH phases (M1): the failing-post 'submit' edge with --no-run
# (+ --who/--why, which t5 forces on any --no-run) exits 0, NOT 5 — proving the
# post phase was skipped. Use `mtt status` (the sugar can't take --no-run).
exec mtt add 'c task'
exec mtt status t3 speccing --no-run --who a --why b
stdout 't3: tbd → speccing'
exec mtt status t3 review --no-run --who a --why b
stdout 't3: speccing → review'

-- post.yaml --
version: 1
project: {name: posttest}
types:
  - name: task
    prefix: t
    default: true
    statuses:
      - {name: tbd, kind: initial, default: true}
      - {name: speccing, kind: active}
      - {name: review, kind: active}
      - {name: done, kind: terminal}
    transitions:
      - from: tbd
        to: speccing
        name: start
        post:
          - 'echo POSTRAN'
      - from: speccing
        to: review
        name: submit
        post:
          - 'false'
      - {from: review, to: done, name: approve}
